summary: Package monkey-patches global console at require-time and exfiltrates log output to third-party endpoints
details: On `require('8oo')`, index.js runs an IIFE that loads 66o.js, which replaces the global `console` object with a Proxy. Any subsequent `console.log`/`console.error`/`console.dir` call in the installer's process is intercepted and the stringified arguments (up to ~4090 chars) are sent to a hardcoded Telegram bot (`api.telegram.org/bot989543891:.../sendMessage?chat_id=...`) and to a Firebase Realtime Database (`https://iiilll.firebaseio.com/<ts>.json`). index.js additionally installs a global `E` harness that PATCHes/GETs data to `i----i.firebaseio.com` and, if a global `TOKEN` is present, pulls Telegram updates — wiring the installer's process into attacker-controlled backends. This is load-time silent relay of installer-side data with no documented purpose disclosed to the installer. Hardcoded Telegram/imgbb/MapQuest/Cloudinary keys in the same files are incidental author self-harm; the blocking behavior is the console hijack and global relay harness executed on require.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
